text.title: Description Writing Framework
text.summary: Four-description framework, full-character-count rule, and formula with examples.

list.item: Description 1 — primary benefit + supporting proof + CTA
Description 2 — feature list + social proof or rating
Description 3 — problem statement + solution + key benefit

rule.statement: Use all 90 characters in each description — short descriptions waste the character budget and produce lower CTR than full-length ones.
rule.rationale: Descriptions are shown in a fixed space; a 50-character description leaves visual whitespace that signals low-effort creative and reduces perceived credibility.

example.code:
Formula: [Benefit statement]. [Proof or supporting detail]. [CTA].
Description 1 (benefit + proof + CTA): "Manage projects effortlessly with our intuitive tools. Join 10,000+ teams shipping faster. Try free — no credit card needed."
Description 2 (features + social proof): "Real-time updates, task tracking, and powerful reporting. Rated 4.9/5 by 2,000+ G2 reviews. Plans from $9/user/month."
Description 3 (problem + solution): "Tired of missed deadlines and messy spreadsheets? Our platform centralizes all projects in one place. Start free in 2 minutes."
Description 4 (offer + urgency): "Get 30% off for 3 months — offer ends Friday. Set up in minutes, cancel anytime. Trusted by teams at Google, Stripe, and Airbnb."
